sections.heading: About this Task
sections.body:
Atlas provides different modes to create aggregation pipelines:

- Stage View Mode, a visual pipeline editor that preloads pipeline syntax based
  on your selected stages.

- Stage Wizard, a feature of Stage View Mode that provides a set of templates
  for simple aggregation stage use cases. The Stage Wizard only includes
  simple use cases to help you get started with your aggregation pipeline.

- Focus Mode, a feature of Stage View Mode where you edit one pipeline stage
  at a time. Focus Mode helps you manage complex or deeply nested aggregation
  pipeline stages.

- Text View Mode, a text-based pipeline editor that accepts raw
  pipeline syntax.

sections.heading: Before You Begin
sections.body:
To build an aggregation pipeline, choose a collection and click the
**Aggregations** tab. Atlas displays a blank
aggregation pipeline. The **Preview of Documents in the
Collection** section shows 10 documents randomly sampled from the chosen
collection.

> **Note:** 
>
> MongoDB Search Stages
>
> When you connect to a MongoDB deployment hosted on
> [Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/cloud/atlas),
> Atlas-only stages [$search](atlas/reference/atlas-search/query-syntax)
> and [$searchMeta](atlas/reference/atlas-search/query-syntax)
> become available in the Aggregation Pipeline Builder. Use these stages
> to perform [full-text search](atlas/atlas-search/atlas-search-overview)
> on Atlas collections.

sections.heading: Steps
sections.body:
To see how to create an aggregation pipeline, select the tab corresponding to
your chosen view mode:**Stage:**Stage View Mode

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the  view
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the aggregation pipeline pane, ensure the
  **\{} Stages** toggle switch is selected.

![Toggle on stage view mode](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-stageview-toggle-on.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Add an aggregation stage
sections.body:
At the bottom of the aggregation pipeline pane, click the
**+ Add Stage** button.

sections.heading: Step 3: Select an aggregation pipeline stage
sections.body:
On the upper-left corner of the aggregation stage card,
click the **Select** drop-down menu and select the
[aggregation pipeline stage](manual/reference/mql/aggregation-stages) to use for the first
stage of the pipeline.

sections.heading: Step 4: Fill in your pipeline stage
sections.body:
Fill in your selected stage. You can adjust the width of the
pipeline stage by dragging its border to the right.

> **Note:** 
>
> The toggle to the right of each pipeline stage name dictates whether that
> stage is included in the pipeline. Toggling a pipeline stage also updates the
> pipeline preview, which updates based on whether or not that stage is included.
>
> For example, the following pipeline excludes the first
> [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match) stage
> and only includes the
> [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project)
> stage:
>
> ![Aggregation Builder exclude stage example](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-exclude-stage-example.png)

sections.heading: Step 5: Add additional pipeline stages
sections.body:
To add an additional pipeline stage after your last aggregation
stage, click **Add Stage**. To add an aggregation stage
before your most recently added stage, click the plus **+**
icon above the stage card.

Repeat steps 3 and 4 for each additional stage.

> **Note:** You can change the order of pipeline stages by dragging
> the header of each stage card.

sections.heading: Step 6: Run the pipeline
sections.body:
At the top-right corner of the pipeline builder, click
**Run**. Atlas returns your results in the
document view.> **Warning:** 
>
> Some aggregation operators, like [$merge](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/merge) and
> [$out](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/out), can modify your collection's data.
>
> If your aggregation pipeline contains operators that can modify
> your collection's data, you are prompted for confirmation before
> the pipeline is executed.**Wizard:**Stage Wizard

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the  view
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the aggregation pipeline pane, ensure the
  **\{} Stages** toggle switch is selected.

![Toggle on stage view mode](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-stageview-toggle-on.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Open the  card
sections.body:
To the right of the view mode toggle, click the wand icon to open
the **Stage Wizard** card.

![Click the icon to the right of the view mode toggle.](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-stage-wizard.png)

sections.heading: Step 3: (Optional) Search for an aggregation use case
sections.body:
On the **Stage Wizard** card, you can filter the
use cases by searching for keywords associated with the use case or
aggregation stage.

sections.heading: Step 4: Select an aggregation pipeline stage use case
sections.body:
On the **Stage Wizard** card, select a stage use
case for the first stage of your pipeline. You can click the stage
card to add it to the end of your pipeline or drag it to your
preferred position.

After you select a use case, Atlas populates the stage
card with a form that corresponds to the selected aggregation
pipeline stage.

The Stage Wizard use cases include the following aggregation stages:

- [$group](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/group)

- [$lookup](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/lookup)

- [$match](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/match)

- [$project](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/project)

- [$sort](manual/reference/operator/aggregation/sort)

sections.heading: Step 5: Fill in your pipeline stage
sections.body:
Fill in the form for your selected stage and click
**Apply**. After you click **Apply**, the form will
turn into a stage card that you can edit in Stage View Mode, Focus
Mode, or Text View Mode.

Atlas populates the Stage Output with up to ten sample
output documents.

> **Note:** You cannot edit an existing stage through the Stage Wizard. The
> Stage Wizard can only add new stages. To edit an existing stage,
> use Stage View Mode, Focus Mode, or Text View Mode.

sections.heading: Step 6: Add additional pipeline stages
sections.body:
To add more aggregation stages to your pipeline, repeat steps 3
and 4 for each additional stage.

> **Tip:** You can change the order of pipeline stages by dragging the
> header of each stage card.

sections.heading: Step 7: Run the pipeline
sections.body:
At the top-right corner of the pipeline builder, click
**Run**. Atlas returns your results in the
document view.**Focus:**Focus Mode

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the  view
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

\#. In the aggregation pipeline pane, ensure the
**\{} Stages** toggle switch is selected.

![Toggle on stage view mode](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-stageview-toggle-on.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Add an aggregation stage
sections.body:
If you have not already created an aggregation stage, click the
**+ Add Stage** button at the bottom of the aggregation
pipeline pane.

sections.heading: Step 3: Open Focus Mode
sections.body:
On the upper-right corner of the stage card, click the
Focus Mode icon.

![Select the Focus Mode button](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/focus-mode-button.png)

sections.heading: Step 4: Select an aggregation pipeline stage
sections.body:
Click the **Select** drop-down menu and select the
[aggregation pipeline stage](manual/reference/mql/aggregation-stages) to use for the first
stage of the pipeline.

sections.heading: Step 5: Fill in your pipeline stage
sections.body:
Fill in your selected  stage. Atlas populates the
**Stage Output** with up to ten sample output documents.

You can adjust the width of the **Stage Input**, stage
editor, and the **Stage Output** by dragging their border to
the desired size.

> **Note:** 
>
> The toggle to the right of each pipeline stage name dictates whether that
> stage is included in the pipeline. Toggling a pipeline stage also updates the
> pipeline preview, which updates based on whether or not that stage is included.

sections.heading: Step 6: Add additional pipeline stages
sections.body:
Click the **Add Stage** dropdown to add additional
aggregation stages before or after your last aggregation stage.
Repeat steps 4 and 5 for each additional stage.

You can add stages with the following keyboard shortcuts:

- To add a stage after the current stage:

  - Windows / Linux: **Ctrl + Shift + A**

  - Mac: **⌘ + Shift + A**

- To add a stage before the current stage:

  - Windows / Linux: **Ctrl + Shift + B**

  - Mac: **⌘ + Shift + B**

sections.heading: Step 7: Navigate between stages
sections.body:
To navigate between different stages, select the stage you
want to edit from the **Stage** dropdown in the upper-left
corner of the Focus Mode modal.

You can navigate between stages with the following keyboard
shortcuts:

- To go to the stage before the current stage:

  - Windows / Linux: **Ctrl + Shift + 9**

  - Mac: **⌘ + Shift + 9**

- To add a stage before the current stage:

  - Windows / Linux: **Ctrl + Shift + 0**

  - Mac: **⌘ + Shift + 0**

sections.heading: Step 8: Run the pipeline
sections.body:
Click **x** to exit Focus Mode and select
**Run** at the top right of the pipeline builder.
Atlas returns your results in the document view.**Text:**Text View Mode

sections.heading: Step 1: Select the  view
sections.body:
- Select the collection.

- In the aggregation pipeline pane, click the
  **\</> Text** toggle switch to enable text mode
  for pipeline editing.

![Toggle textmode on](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-textview-toggle-on.png)

sections.heading: Step 2: Enter your aggregation pipeline
sections.body:
Enter valid aggregation syntax into the text editor. The text
editor provides real-time linting for correct syntax and debugging
information.

You can also use Text View Mode to import aggregation pipelines from
plain text by typing or pasting your pipeline into the text editor.

For example, following pipeline limits the query results to `4`
documents.

```javascript
[ { "$limit" : 4 } ]
```

> **Note:** To expand all embedded fields and documents within the preview
> results, click **Output Options** and select
> **Expand all fields**.
>
> ![Pipeline Output preview](/atlas-ui/images/atlas-ui/compass/agg-builder-textview-expand-pipeline-preview.png)

sections.heading: Step 3: Run the pipeline
sections.body:
Click **Run** at the top right of the pipeline
builder. Atlas returns your results in the document view.
